---
title: "quality_coding_ageyouth"
author: "Prerana"
date: "2025-09-11"
output: html_document
---
```{r Load packages}
library(readxl)             # Loads package for reading Excel files
library(metafor)            # Loads metafor package for conducting meta-analyses
library(psych)              # Loads psych package for descriptive statistics and psychometrics
library(ggplot2)            # Loads ggplot2 for creating visualizations
library(patchwork)          # Loads patchwork to combine multiple ggplots into one
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)

theme_update(              # Updates global ggplot2 theme settings
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),            # Center-aligns plot titles
  axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"),        # Sets x-axis text color to black
  axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")         # Sets y-axis text color to black
)

```


```{r Data read}
# Step 1: Read Excel file with original column names
data_youth_qc <- read_xlsx("data/ageYouth_qualitycode_.xlsx", skip = 1)
head(data_youth_qc)

```

```{r data cleaning}


# Clean the data
dat_clean <- data_youth_qc %>%
  # Drop unwanted columns
  select(-`5_Reviewer`, -`4_Agre`) %>%
  # Recode all non-ID/Title columns
  mutate(across(
    -c(`0_IDF`, `1_ID`, `2_Author`, `3_Year`, `4_Title`, `Rep_Rep`),
    ~ case_when(
      . %in% c("yes", "Yes") ~ 0,
      . %in% c("no", "No") ~ 1,
      . %in% c("9999", "unable to determine") ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ))

# Inspect result
head(dat_clean)

```


```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Save cleaned data to Excel
output_file <- "output/quality_coding_clean.xlsx"
write.xlsx(dat_clean, output_file, rowNames = FALSE)

cat("File saved as:", output_file)

```
